title: Compare an Operator-Reported Aviva Action Against WCS or OptiSweep System State
summary: Use operator-reported information from Aviva and compare it against the state visible in WCS or OptiSweep to determine whether an Aviva-issued action, such as startup, is reflected in backend system state and to identify a likely communication mismatch.
when_to_use: Use this diagnostic when an operator reports that an action was performed in Aviva, or that Aviva shows a particular status, and support needs to verify whether WCS or OptiSweep reflects the same state. This is especially applicable when support users do not have direct Aviva access.
not_for: Do not use this procedure as a direct method to fix Aviva-side issues.
Do not use this procedure to claim confirmed root cause when only a state mismatch has been observed.
Do not use this procedure when direct Aviva interaction is required but support does not have Aviva access.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Ask the operator what action was performed in Aviva, review WCS or OptiSweep state, compare the reported and observed states, and document any mismatch.
Report what action was performed in Aviva and what status is visible in Aviva when support does not have direct access.

safety_notes: This source describes a diagnostic comparison activity only; it does not authorize changing Aviva, WCS, or OptiSweep state.
If support does not have Aviva access, rely on the operator to report what is visible in Aviva and note that limitation.
If a mismatch is observed, document it as an observed communication issue for follow-up rather than assuming support can directly correct Aviva.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to WCS or OptiSweep system state
Operator assistance to report what is visible in Aviva
Knowledge of the reported action sent from Aviva

access_required: Operator communication for Aviva status reporting when support lacks Aviva access
system_preconditions: An operator-reported Aviva action or Aviva status is available for comparison

visual_references.description: Training video frame and transcript segment covering Aviva access limitations for support users and the instruction to compare Aviva-reported actions against WCS or OptiSweep state.

steps.title: Collect the operator-reported Aviva action and status
steps.purpose: Establish what action was reportedly performed in Aviva and what status Aviva is showing before comparing backend state.
steps.instruction: Ask the operator what action was performed in Aviva, such as pressing a startup-related control, and ask what status they currently see in Aviva. If you do not have Aviva access, rely on the operator to report what is visible and note that limitation.
steps.expected_result: You have a clear operator-reported Aviva action and current Aviva status to use as the comparison baseline.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can describe the Aviva action and visible status clearly enough to support comparison.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot confirm what action was taken or what Aviva currently shows, or support assumes Aviva state without operator confirmation.

actions.label: Ask operator for Aviva action
actions.exact_action: Ask the operator what action was performed in Aviva, such as pressing a startup-related control, and what status they are seeing in Aviva.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Transcript-supported guidance that non-UPS support users may not have Aviva access and should direct the operator on where to look in Aviva.

stop_or_escalate_if: Support cannot access Aviva and the operator cannot provide reliable Aviva status details.
The reported Aviva action is too vague to compare against WCS or OptiSweep state.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Anybody who's not a UPS user doesn't have access to Aviva. The best thing you will have, you'll have an operator who you can tell where to look in Aviva.

steps.title: Check WCS or OptiSweep for current system state
steps.purpose: Determine the backend system state after the reported Aviva action.
steps.instruction: Use WCS or OptiSweep to check the current state of the system after the reported Aviva action.
steps.expected_result: You can identify the current state shown in WCS or OptiSweep for the condition the operator reported from Aviva.
steps.healthy_condition: WCS or OptiSweep shows a readable current state that can be compared to the operator-reported Aviva action.
steps.failure_condition: WCS or OptiSweep state cannot be viewed, is unclear, or does not provide enough information to compare.

actions.label: Review backend system state
actions.exact_action: Use WCS or OptiSweep to check the current state of the system after the reported Aviva action.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training segment stating that after an Aviva-triggered API or write error, support should check WCS or OptiSweep to see the state of the system.

stop_or_escalate_if: WCS or OptiSweep state is not accessible to support.
The backend state cannot be determined from the available WCS or OptiSweep view.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If you have a write error from Aviva and an API was triggered, the best thing would be to see in WCS or in our OptiSweep what is the state of the system.

steps.title: Compare the reported Aviva state to backend state
steps.purpose: Determine whether the operator-reported Aviva action aligns with the state visible in WCS or OptiSweep.
steps.instruction: Compare the operator-reported Aviva action and status to the state visible in WCS or OptiSweep.
steps.expected_result: You can state whether the backend system reflects the same condition the operator reported from Aviva.
steps.healthy_condition: The reported Aviva action and the WCS or OptiSweep state are consistent.
steps.failure_condition: The reported Aviva action and the WCS or OptiSweep state do not align.

actions.label: Compare reported and observed states
actions.exact_action: Compare the operator-reported Aviva action and status to the state visible in WCS or OptiSweep.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Segment describing comparison of Aviva-issued actions, such as startup, against WCS or OptiSweep state.

stop_or_escalate_if: The operator-reported Aviva state cannot be matched to any observable WCS or OptiSweep state.
The comparison indicates a mismatch that requires follow-up documentation.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If you have a write error from Aviva and an API was triggered, the best thing would be to see in WCS or in our OptiSweep what is the state of the system.

steps.title: Verify whether the expected state change is present
steps.purpose: Confirm whether the specific expected state change, such as startup, is actually reflected in backend state.
steps.instruction: Verify whether the expected state change is present, such as whether startup is reflected in WCS or OptiSweep.
steps.expected_result: You confirm either that the expected state change is present or that it is absent in backend state.
steps.healthy_condition: The expected state change reported from Aviva is visible in WCS or OptiSweep.
steps.failure_condition: The expected state change reported from Aviva is not visible in WCS or OptiSweep.

actions.label: Confirm expected backend state change
actions.exact_action: Verify whether the expected state change is present, such as whether startup is reflected in WCS or OptiSweep.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Example in the training that if Aviva put the system in startup, support should verify whether startup is visible in WCS or OptiSweep.

stop_or_escalate_if: The expected state change is absent in WCS or OptiSweep after the operator reports it was issued in Aviva.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If Aviva put it in startup, but we don't see any startup, it's a communication issue.

steps.title: Document any mismatch as an observed communication issue
steps.purpose: Record the observed discrepancy without overstating root cause or implying direct Aviva remediation capability.
steps.instruction: If the reported Aviva action is not reflected in WCS or OptiSweep, document the mismatch as an observed communication issue rather than assuming support can directly fix Aviva.
steps.expected_result: The mismatch is documented clearly as an observed communication issue for follow-up.
steps.healthy_condition: Observed mismatch is captured accurately, including the limitation that support may not have direct Aviva access or direct Aviva remediation capability.
steps.failure_condition: The issue is undocumented, or support claims a confirmed root cause or direct Aviva fix without source support.

actions.label: Record communication mismatch
actions.exact_action: If the reported Aviva action is not reflected in WCS or OptiSweep, document the mismatch as an observed communication issue rather than assuming support can directly fix Aviva.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training guidance that a startup mismatch between Aviva and WCS or OptiSweep indicates a communication issue and that support may not be able to directly fix Aviva.

stop_or_escalate_if: A mismatch is observed between Aviva-reported action and WCS or OptiSweep state.
The issue appears to be on the Aviva side and support cannot directly remediate it.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If Aviva put it in startup, but we don't see any startup, it's a communication issue.
Anybody who's not a UPS user doesn't have access to Aviva. The best thing you will have, you'll have an operator who you can tell where to look in Aviva.

success_criteria: The operator-reported Aviva action and status are captured.
The current state in WCS or OptiSweep is reviewed.
Support determines whether the backend state matches the reported Aviva action.
Any mismatch is documented as an observed communication issue for follow-up.
healthy_conditions: The operator can report what is visible in Aviva.
WCS or OptiSweep provides a visible current system state.
The expected state change, such as startup, is reflected in WCS or OptiSweep when reported from Aviva.
failure_conditions: Support lacks Aviva access and the operator cannot provide usable Aviva details.
WCS or OptiSweep state cannot be determined.
The reported Aviva action is not reflected in WCS or OptiSweep.
Support overstates the finding as a confirmed root cause or direct Aviva fix.
escalation_guidance: If support does not have Aviva access, rely on the operator to report what is visible in Aviva and note that limitation.
If the observed state in WCS or OptiSweep does not match the operator-reported Aviva action, capture the mismatch for follow-up rather than claiming a confirmed root cause.
If the issue appears to be Aviva-side, note that the source states there may be nothing support can do to directly fix Aviva.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary source segment for Aviva access limitations and WCS/OptiSweep state comparison guidance.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Anybody who's not a UPS user doesn't have access to Aviva. The best thing you will have, you'll have an operator who you can tell where to look in Aviva.
If you have a write error from Aviva and an API was triggered, the best thing would be to see in WCS or in our OptiSweep what is the state of the system.
If Aviva put it in startup, but we don't see any startup, it's a communication issue.

missing_details: The source does not specify the exact WCS or OptiSweep screen name to use for viewing system state.
The source does not provide exact field names, buttons, or navigation steps inside WCS or OptiSweep for this comparison.
The source does not provide a formal documentation template for recording the mismatch.
The source gives startup as an example but does not provide a broader validated list of Aviva actions to compare.
The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
